219: pub fn push_or_update_pr(
220:     state: &mut StackState,
221:     branch: &str,
222:     parent: &str,
223:     draft: bool,
224:     title_override: Option<&str>,
225:     body_override: Option<&str>,
226:     body_explicitly_set: bool,
227:     repo_override: Option<&str>,
228: ) -> Result<String> {
229:     // Normalize repo shorthand (e.g. "fork" → "owner/repo" from remote URL).
230:     let resolved_override: Option<String> =
231:         repo_override.map(|s| github::resolve_repo_shorthand(s));
232:     // Resolve effective repo: CLI flag > stored per-branch > global config > None
233:     let effective_repo: Option<String> = resolved_override
234:         .clone()
235:         .or_else(|| state.effective_pr_repo(branch));
236: 
237:     // Resolve the push remote for cross-fork head prefix.
238:     let push_remote = state.effective_push_remote(branch);
239: 
240:     // Collect upstream ancestor PRs for the stack section.
241:     // path_to_trunk returns [branch, ..., trunk]; we want ancestors only.
242:     let ancestors = stack_ancestors(state, branch, &github::repo_name().unwrap_or_default());
243: 
244:     let existing_pr = github::get_pr_status_in_repo(branch, effective_repo.as_deref())?;
245: 
246:     let pr_url = match existing_pr {
247:         Some(pr) => {
248:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_number = Some(pr.number);
249: 
250:             // Update PR base only when the stack parent is genuinely an ancestor of this branch.
251:             // If the branch was rebased onto a different base outside of ez (bypassing stack
252:             // metadata), is_ancestor returns false and we leave the PR base alone so we don't
253:             // clobber a manual `gh pr edit --base` change.
254:             if pr.base != parent {
255:                 if git::is_ancestor(parent, branch) {
256:                     if let Err(e) = github::update_pr_base_in_repo(
257:                         pr.number,
258:                         parent,
259:                         effective_repo.as_deref(),
260:                     ) {
261:                         ui::warn(&format!(
262:                             "Push succeeded but PR #{} base could not be updated to `{parent}`: {e}",
263:                             pr.number
264:                         ));
265:                     } else {
266:                         ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{} base to `{parent}`", pr.number));
267:                     }
268:                 } else {
269:                     ui::warn(&format!(
270:                         "PR #{} base not updated: `{parent}` is not an ancestor of `{branch}` \
271:                          (stack metadata may be stale — run `ez sync` or update manually)",
272:                         pr.number
273:                     ));
274:                 }
275:             }
276: 
277:             // Only update body if user explicitly passed --body/--body-file.
278:             if body_explicitly_set {
279:                 let raw_body = body_override.unwrap_or("Part of a stack managed by `ez`.");
280:                 let body = crate::stack_body::build_stack_body(&ancestors, raw_body);
281:                 if let Err(e) = github::edit_pr_in_repo(
282:                     pr.number,
283:                     title_override,
284:                     Some(&body),
285:                     effective_repo.as_deref(),
286:                 ) {
287:                     ui::warn(&format!(
288:                         "Push succeeded but PR #{} could not be updated: {e}",
289:                         pr.number
290:                     ));
291:                 } else {
292:                     ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{}", pr.number));
293:                 }
294:             } else if title_override.is_some() {
295:                 if let Err(e) =
296:                     github::edit_pr_in_repo(pr.number, title_override, None, effective_repo.as_deref())
297:                 {
298:                     ui::warn(&format!(
299:                         "Push succeeded but PR #{} title could not be updated: {e}",
300:                         pr.number
301:                     ));
302:                 } else {
303:                     ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{} title", pr.number));
304:                 }
305:             }
306: 
307:             pr.url
308:         }
309:         None => {
310:             // Derive the PR title from the first commit on this branch.
311:             let range = format!("{parent}..{branch}");
312:             let commits = git::log_oneline(&range, 1)?;
313:             let derived_title = commits
314:                 .first()
315:                 .map(|(_, msg)| msg.clone())
316:                 .unwrap_or_else(|| branch.to_string());
317: 
318:             let title = title_override.unwrap_or(&derived_title);
319:             let default_body = "Part of a stack managed by `ez`.";
320:             let raw_body = body_override.unwrap_or(default_body);
321: 
322:             // Always append stack section to new PRs.
323:             let body = crate::stack_body::build_stack_body(&ancestors, raw_body);
324: 
325:             // Compute cross-fork --head value.
326:             let head = github::cross_fork_head(branch, &push_remote, effective_repo.as_deref());
327: 
328:             let pr = github::create_pr_in_repo(
329:                 title,
330:                 &body,
331:                 parent,
332:                 &head,
333:                 draft,
334:                 effective_repo.as_deref(),
335:             )?;
336:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_number = Some(pr.number);
337:             // Only persist pr_repo when --repo was explicitly passed on the CLI.
338:             if let Some(ref r) = resolved_override {
339:                 state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_repo = Some(r.clone());
340:             }
341:             ui::info(&format!("Created PR #{}: {}", pr.number, pr.url));
342:             pr.url
343:         }
344:     };
345: 
346:     Ok(pr_url)
347: }
348:

357:         state.add_branch("feat/a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
358:         state.add_branch("feat/b", "feat/a", "bbb", None, None);
